originalTitle: Ex-keeper Manninger dies after car hit by train
originalDescription: Former Arsenal goalkeeper Alex Manninger died at the age of 48 after his car was struck by a train.

originalTitle: Bellingham says Camavinga red 'a joke' as Real fury grows
originalDescription: Real Madrid's Jude Bellingham says the decision to send off team-mate Eduardo Camavinga during their 4-3 Champions League loss to Bayern Munich was "a joke".

originalTitle: Fifa blamed for $100 World Cup trains from New York
originalDescription: New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill accuses Fifa of failing to provide funding, which means fans must foot the bill for high transport costs at the World Cup this summer.

originalTitle: Captain Silva to leave Man City at end of season
originalDescription: Manchester City captain Bernardo Silva will leave the club when his contract expires at the end of this season.

originalTitle: How Wubben-Moy has stepped into Williamson's shoes
originalDescription: England defender Lotte Wubben-Moy took her chance when she was handed a rare start in Tuesday's victory over Spain in Leah Williamson's absence.

originalTitle: Seven goals, fastest strike and two red cards - Bayern-Real delivers drama
originalDescription: The quickest strike of this season's Champions League, seven goals, two red cards and the 15-time winners exiting in anger - once again the knockout stages of Europe's elite tournament delivered drama in spades.

originalTitle: Ekitike to miss rest of season and World Cup with ruptured Achilles
originalDescription: France striker Hugo Ekitike will miss the World Cup after suffering a rupture of the Achilles tendon during Liverpool's Champions League defeat by Paris St-Germain on Tuesday.

originalTitle: Play-off pending? How a draw at the Etihad could set up thrilling finale
originalDescription: Man City's meeting against Arsenal may go a long way to deciding the destination of the Premier League trophy in May, but how close a finish could it be?

originalTitle: MVG could face O’Connor as he chases third European Darts Grand Prix crown
originalDescription: Michael van Gerwen could face 'The Magpie' William O’Connor as he chases a third Elten Safety Shoes European Darts Grand Prix crown in Sindelfingen.

originalTitle: Rice: Arsenal ready for 'ultimate test' at Man City
originalDescription: Declan Rice insists Arsenal are ready to eradicate the hurt from their Carabao Cup final defeat by making a "massive statement" in what he described as the "ultimate test" against Man City at the Etihad on Sunday.

originalTitle: Golf leaderboards: Latest scores from RBC Heritage and more
originalDescription: All the latest golf scores and results from around the world, including the RBC Heritage on the PGA Tour and the LET's Joburg Ladies Open.

originalTitle: Baumgardner on Taylor, Serrano and Mayer: 'Let’s get them all!'
originalDescription: Alycia Baumgardner will chase fights with the biggest names in the sport.

originalTitle: England's Botterman and Campbell ruled out of Women's Six Nations
originalDescription: England's squad depth continues to be tested after front rows Hannah Botterman and May Campbell became the latest players to be ruled out of the Guinness Women's Six Nations.
We have a busy day’s racing on Thursday with action from Hereford and Ripon, live on Sky Sports Racing...

originalTitle: Real Madrid’s Champions League exit will bring big change at the Bernabeu
originalDescription: Real Madrid's Champions League elimination by Bayern Munich will surely provoke another period of upheaval at the Spanish side, starting with Alvaro Arbeloa's position as head coach.

originalTitle: LIV Golf season to continue 'full throttle' amid shutdown reports, says CEO
originalDescription: LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil sought to quell speculation about the league's financial future with a memo to his staff that said the 2026 season will continue as planned without interruption and "at full throttle".
